title: Intensely Gooey Japanese Rhubarb Shoyu Ramen
description: A rich and savory Japanese ramen dish featuring rhubarb and a gooey shoyu sauce, perfect for a crowd-pleasing dinner. This keto-friendly recipe is sure to satisfy your cravings.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the coconut oil over medium heat and cook the pork belly until browned and crispy.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken broth to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the shoyu sauce to the pot and stir to combine, then simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
In a separate pan, cook the rhubarb over medium heat until tender and caramelized, stirring occasionally.
To assemble the ramen, place a portion of the pork belly and rhubarb into a bowl, then ladle the hot broth over the top.
Garnish with green onions, sesame seeds, and a soft-boiled egg, then serve immediately.
To emulsify the sauce, whisk in a small amount of xanthan gum or guar gum before serving.
